Your Name

Mathematics & Computer Science Student · Montpellier, France · your.email@example.com

Final year Master's student in Applied Mathematics (MANU - Modelling And Numerical Analysis) at University of Montpellier. Graduate of École 42 Paris and holder of a Bachelor's in General Mathematics. Passionate about computational methods, numerical analysis, and scientific computing. Currently seeking a 6-month internship opportunity in computational mathematics, numerical analysis, or scientific software development.

Mathematical & Computational Projects

1D Euler Multiphase Methods

Advanced Numerical Methods Implementation

Developed sophisticated C++ implementations of Discontinuous Galerkin (DG) and DGSEM methods for solving 1D Euler equations. Features entropy-stable schemes, Runge-Kutta time integration, and comprehensive convergence analysis. Includes Rusanov numerical flux, Gauss-Legendre quadrature, and advanced boundary condition handling.

Technologies: C++20, Eigen, CMake, Python (visualization), OpenMP

2024

Computational Fluid Dynamics Solver

High-Order Numerical Schemes

Implementation of entropy-stable DGSEM methods for gas dynamics problems including shock tube simulations (Toro test cases). Features polynomial basis functions, L2 projection methods, and advanced visualization of convergence rates.

Key Features: Chandrashekar entropy flux, Ismail-Roe schemes, periodic boundary conditions

2023-2024

Mathematical Analysis Projects

Theoretical & Numerical Studies

Various projects in pure and applied mathematics including differential equations, functional analysis, and numerical optimization. Developed computational tools for solving complex mathematical problems using modern scientific computing approaches.

Areas: PDE analysis, optimization theory, statistical modeling, algorithm design

2021-2024

Mathematical Documentation with Quarto

Reproducible Research & Technical Writing

Comprehensive documentation of mathematical methods and numerical analysis using Quarto. Includes theoretical foundations, algorithm implementation details, and convergence analysis with interactive mathematical content and computational examples.

Key Documents: Discontinuous Galerkin Methods for 1D Euler Equations, Mathematical Foundations of Numerical Methods

View Euler Methods Analysis    View Mathematical Foundations

2023-2024

Academic & Professional Experience

Research Assistant

University of Montpellier - MANU Program

Working on advanced numerical methods for multiphase flow problems. Developing high-order schemes for hyperbolic conservation laws with applications to computational fluid dynamics. Contributing to research in entropy-stable methods and discontinuous Galerkin formulations.

2023 - Present

Teaching Assistant

University of Montpellier - Mathematics Department

Assisted in teaching undergraduate mathematics courses including calculus, linear algebra, and numerical analysis. Provided tutoring and support for students in mathematical problem-solving and computational methods.

2022 - 2023

Software Development Projects

École 42 Paris

Completed intensive programming projects focusing on system-level programming, algorithms, and data structures. Developed strong foundation in C/C++ programming and collaborative software development using Git and modern development practices.

2019 - 2021

University of Montpellier

Master's Degree in Applied Mathematics
MANU (Modelling And Numerical Analysis)

Focus: Computational fluid dynamics, numerical methods for PDEs, scientific computing, high-performance computing, and mathematical modeling of physical phenomena

Comprehensive foundation in pure and applied mathematics including analysis, algebra, probability, statistics, and differential equations

Research Interests & Career Goals

Research Areas

My primary research interests lie at the intersection of applied mathematics and computational science. I am particularly passionate about developing and implementing advanced numerical methods for solving partial differential equations, with applications to fluid dynamics and multiphase flows.

I am fascinated by the mathematical elegance of entropy-stable schemes and high-order methods like Discontinuous Galerkin formulations. My work focuses on creating robust, efficient computational tools that can tackle complex physical phenomena while maintaining mathematical rigor.

Career Objectives

Currently seeking a 6-month internship opportunity where I can apply my mathematical and computational skills to real-world problems. I am interested in positions involving:

  • Computational fluid dynamics and numerical simulation
  • Scientific computing in industrial applications
  • Mathematical modeling of physical systems
  • High-performance computing and algorithm optimization
  • Research and development in academic or industrial settings
Personal Interests

Beyond mathematics and computing, I enjoy exploring the philosophical foundations of mathematics, reading about the history of scientific discoveries, and staying current with advances in computational science and machine learning. I also appreciate the collaborative nature of open-source scientific software development.
